key_insight: LiST is a materials data platform that automates capture, curation, analysis, and sharing across the full sample lifetime, linking experiments, theory, and publication-ready datasets.
engineering_relevance: It gives materials facilities a scalable way to organize high-volume synthesis and characterization data, enabling reproducible workflows, faster iteration, and downstream AI/ML use.
method: Automated capture of experimental and modeling data from synthesis to characterization and simulation
Curates data into searchable sample records and publication data packages with DOI assignment
Provides REST API access for sharing and integration with external tools
evidence_signal: The platform currently hosts synthesis and property data for approximately twenty thousand samples produced with multiple growth techniques.
watch_item: Broad adoption will depend on sustained metadata quality, workflow integration, and consistent curation across diverse experimental groups.
paper_title: Lifetime Sample Tracking (LiST): A Data Platform for Materials Science
